title: execute_enter_worktree mutates process global cwd from sync function
description:
## Severity
Critical

## Dimension
Concurrency / Tell-Dont-Ask / Decoupling

## Location
src/tools/worktree.rs:101-110 (execute_enter_worktree), 134, 213, 244, 255-256 (execute_exit_worktree)

## Violation
The function unconditionally mutates process-global state with std::env::set_current_dir. set_current_dir is process-global. Every other tokio task in the runtime sees the new cwd. The READ_TRACKER uses canonicalize, which depends on cwd for relative paths. The PROJECT_ROOT static was captured at startup specifically to prevent this drift (see mod.rs:78-82 comment: Pinned at startup so that later cds via the worktree tool cannot move the jail underneath us). The jail design ANTICIPATES this anti-pattern but does not prevent it — the worktree tool deliberately violates the assumption.

Worse, exit_worktree calls set_current_dir multiple times (lines 213, 244, 255), and the failure paths (lines 102-110) acknowledge that set_current_dir CAN fail but still report success on the happy path without verifying the cd actually took effect.

The test harness has to serialize tests via cwd_lock (line 349) precisely because of this — production code has no such guard. Concurrent agent sessions WILL race on cwd.

## Mandated refactor
1. Do NOT call set_current_dir at all. Track the worktree cwd as a per-session value in SessionState and pass it explicitly to every tool that needs a working directory (Command::current_dir(...), file resolve paths, etc.).
2. If the design truly requires a per-session cwd notion, scope it per-Session via a Session struct member, not via process globals.
3. Remove the WorktreeState struct (it captures original_cwd as a PathBuf but is never used to restore — dead YAGNI code).

## Filed from QA review dispatch
